lead.title: ‘We Will All Die From Ebola Here’: Virus Stalks Crowded Camps in Congo
lead.snippet: There are a million people displaced by violence in the epicenter. They live in dangerous conditions and are fighting the virus with very little clean water.

items.title: Why I Sought Out a Taliban Commander I Had Fought

items.title: U.S.-Saudi Deal Prompts Fears of a Global Nuclear Arms Race

items.title: Japan’s Leader Invokes Her Hero, Shinzo Abe, to Drive Nationalist Agenda
items.snippet: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi is leveraging her ties to Mr. Abe, the slain Japanese leader, as she pushes to reshape defense and the economy.

items.title: U.S. Military Presence in Jordan Makes It Iranian Target
items.snippet: Relying heavily on U.S. aid and protection, Jordan is unlikely to restrict the American military presence there, despite escalating Iranian airstrikes.

items.title: Why Are Young People in India Protesting?

items.title: How the Trump Administration’s Nuclear Deal With Saudi Arabia May Work
items.snippet: The Trump administration announced an agreement with Saudi Arabia on a civilian nuclear program. Such deals, known as 123 Agreements, are designed to prevent the development of weapons.

items.title: Louvre Gallery Reopens After Brazen Jewel Heist
items.snippet: Thieves stole crown jewels from the Apollo Gallery at the Louvre Museum in October, forcing its closure. Tourists finally returned on Wednesday.

items.title: Canadian Wildfire Evacuees Outraged Over Lacking Support and Communication

items.title: Social media addiction lawsuit against Meta is dropped

items.title: SoundCloud acquires decentralized music platform Nina Protocol months after its shutdown

items.title: How OpenAI’s human mistake led to the AI-powered hack on Hugging Face
items.snippet: OpenAI made a mistake setting up what it called a “highly isolated” testing environment and sandbox. According to cybersecurity experts, that human mistake is what made the AI-powered attack on Hugging Face possible.

items.snippet: Anthropic leaped to a $47 billion revenue run rate by May, compared to $9 billion in 2025. It’s the kind of growth that Menlo Ventures’ Matt Murphy says he’s never seen in 25 years of investing

items.title: Monday.com lays off hundreds to focus on AI

lead.title: The Devotion of Willow Smith

items.title: Tate’s New Director Knows Its Glory Days. Can She Bring Them Back?

items.snippet: A new nonprofit wants to streamline gene therapy for diseases often avoided by pharmaceutical companies — making treatment more like a routine procedure than a bespoke drug.

items.title: The Best Genetic Risk Tools Don’t Work Equally for Everyone
items.snippet: Genetic prediction models are poised to revolutionize medicine. But because they have been trained on European DNA, they threaten to widen health care disparities.

items.title: ‘A Sad and Beautiful World’ Review: A Love to Overcome Fear
items.snippet: In Lebanon, a lush romance in uncertain times asks what keeps us human.
